input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<RefreshView IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
             Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        <CollectionView.EmptyView>
            <Label Text="No items found."
                   HorizontalOptions="Center"
                   VerticalOptions="Center" />
        </CollectionView.EmptyView>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    // Triggered whenever SelectedItem changes
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;
        Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { { "Item", value } });
        SelectedItem = null; // reset so the same item can be re-selected
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` → `Items` | Drives the list; use `ObservableCollection<T>` so adds/removes auto-update the UI |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | Enables tap-to-select (one item at a time) |
| `SelectedItem` → `SelectedItem` | Two-way by default — set it back to `null` after navigating so the row deselects |
| `IsRefreshing` → `IsRefreshing` | Controls the refresh spinner; set `false` when done loading |
| `Command` → `RefreshCommand` | Fired on pull-down; `[RelayCommand]` generates `RefreshCommand` from `RefreshAsync` |

**Important notes:**
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops, even on errors.
- Reset `SelectedItem = null` after navigation — otherwise tapping the same item twice won't fire the property change.
- Use `x:DataType` on the `DataTemplate` for compiled bindings (better perf, compile-time safety).
